justification: All pass criteria met: zero-downtime dual-write strategy clearly designed; four phases (shadow write, dual-read, PG primary, PG only) covering backfill, dual-write, read cutover, and decommission; rollback plan at each phase including shadow-writes to MongoDB in Phase 3; MongoDB data shape inconsistency risk explicitly identified (schema analysis job, JSONB overflow column, field-level verification); trade-offs include dual-write latency (~5-15ms) and operational overhead of maintaining two stores.

observations.text: Exceptionally detailed phase-by-phase rollback strategy, including the critical insight of continuing shadow-writes to MongoDB in Phase 3 to ensure safe rollback

Feature flag-driven canary read shifting (1% → 10% → 50% → 100%) provides fine-grained risk control during cutover
Phases numbered 1-4 but a Phase 0 is introduced in implementation guidance, creating slight inconsistency with the four-phase narrative in the executive summary

trials.agent_output_excerpt:
{
  "executive_summary": "Zero-downtime migration architecture for the Order Management domain from MongoDB to PostgreSQL, using a phased Strangler Fig approach with dual-write synchronization. The design introduces a Data Access Abstraction Layer to decouple application code from storage, a Change Data Capture (CDC) pipeline for historical backfill, and a Shadow Traffic Verification system to validate consistency before cutover. At 200 writes/min (~3.3/sec), the throughput is well within Postgr

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Zero-downtime dual-write migration strategy clearly designed with Strangler Fig pattern. (2) Phases clearly defined: schema setup, backfill, dual-write, shadow-read, read cutover to PG, decommission MongoDB. (3) Rollback plan at each phase via feature flags, with MongoDB kept warm during PG_PRIMARY via reverse dual-write, and 30-day retention before decommission. (4) MongoDB data shape inconsistency risk explicitly identified as high severity with pre-migration schema analysis mitigation. (5) Trade-offs include dual-write operational complexity, longer migration timeline, and DLQ overhead.

observations.text: Exceptionally detailed component architecture with clear boundaries — Repository Pattern, DualWriteRepository, DLQ, Consistency Verifier, and Feature Flag Controller are all well-defined
Backfill race condition explicitly addressed with UPSERT + version comparison guards
Concrete operational details: batch size recommendations, estimated backfill time (~13 hours), reading from MongoDB secondary replica
Shadow-read phase (PG_SHADOW_READ) is an excellent addition beyond the rubric requirements — validates query correctness before cutover
Optimistic concurrency with version fields addresses dual-write ordering risk
Pitfalls section provides actionable guardrails including the >99.99% consistency gate before phase advancement
Dual-write latency trade-off could be more explicit — the async secondary write design minimizes but doesn't eliminate latency impact, and this nuance is somewhat buried
